title: Blackwater Systems

{{Quote|The factory was built quickly, with little regard for basic safety practices. Enormous bribes were paid to inspectors to overlook violations that are part of the laws of Falleen.|Mazara, to Obi-Wan Kenobi|<ref name="Jedi Quest: The False Peace" />}}
'''Blackwater Systems''' was a company opened on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]] sometime before [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]] by the criminal [[Granta Omega]] and [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Jenna Zan Arbor]] as a research and development [[Blackwater Systems facility|facility]] for the [[Zone of Self-Containment]] [[Spice/Legends|drug]]. The company was known for ignoring environmental and safety laws, and was the target of a [[Workers Rights group|protest group]] that attempted to expose its illegal actions. In 24 BBY, several [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] infiltrated Blackwater's factory, and Omega chose to destroy it rather than let his work be discovered.

==Description==
{{Quote|That new factory we've heard of—Blackwater Systems. Let's head there. It was build quickly and has a bad reputation among the Falleens.|Obi-Wan Kenobi to Anakin Skywalker|<ref name="Jedi Quest: The False Peace" />}}
Workers at Blackwater Systems wore [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Jumpsuit/Legends|jumpsuits]] called [[unisuit]]s and checked levels on machines that monitored the injection of liquid into canisters. Most of the workers were employed to double-check the machines for mistakes, and the newest workers swept the factory floors before leaving. The managers were supposed to monitor the workers from a tier running around the upper level of the factory floor, but they rarely paid any attention to them. The factory itself was at the end of a long line of factories, located over two [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] from the nearest [[Cloud bus|cloud-bus]] stop. The building had no windows, was made entirely from black [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] and stone, and had an additional wing on one side. Blackwater was well known for ignoring safety regulations and for bribing inspectors to overlook the infractions that led to the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of several employees and was not opposed to using lethal force on civilians.<ref name="Jedi Quest: The False Peace" />

==History==
Sometime before [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> the criminal [[Granta Omega]] and [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Jenna Zan Arbor]] founded a company using the name Blackwater Systems, which opened a factory in the [[Yellow District]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Falleen (planet)/Legends|Falleen]] shortly before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Omega, Zan Arbor, and a deposed dictator from the planet of [[Romin]], [[Roy Teda]], used the [[Blackwater Systems facility]] to test various [[Water/Legends|water]]-borne delivery mechanisms for the [[Zone of Self-Containment]], a [[Spice/Legends|drug]] designed to make those exposed to it too docile to respond to outside stimuli, as part of a plan to expose the drug to the citizens of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="Jedi Quest: The False Peace" />

In 24 BBY,<ref name="Date" /> Blackwater attempted to kill members of a [[Workers Rights group|group of protesters]] as they prepared to violently enter its factory. The attempt was foiled by the intervention of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]], along with their [[Padawan/Legends|apprentices]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Ferus Olin]], as they saved the leader of the protest group, [[Mazara]], from an [[Assassination attempt on Mazara|assassination attempt]]. Mazara then introduced the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to an employment [[Officer/Legends|officer]], [[Wanuri]], who gave them jobs so that they could infiltrate the factory to find Omega. As soon as Omega realized that that Jedi were in the building, he destroyed the facility with explosives in an effort to kill his pursuers. Destroying the facility also eliminated evidence of both the Zone of Self-Containment and a wing of the facility that housed a recreation of a section of the [[Senate utility tunnels]], originally located under the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]] on Coruscant, which was being used to model the effectiveness of the drug if the original tunnels were used to distribute it through either the [[Water/Legends|water]] or [[Atmosphere/Legends|air]] to Coruscant's populace.<ref name="Jedi Quest: The False Peace" />

==Behind the scenes==
Blackwater Systems appeared in the young-adult novel ''[[Jedi Quest: The False Peace]]'', the ninth volume in the [[Star Wars: Jedi Quest|''Jedi Quest'']] series written by [[Jude Watson]].<ref name="Jedi Quest: The False Peace" />
